validation.assistantNameLength: The assistant name must be 3 to 8 characters long.
validation.assistantNameFormat: Use one single word without spaces or special characters.
validation.assistantCalibrationRequired: Please finish the assistant wake-word calibration for the current name and language before saving.

hero.title: Dashboard
hero.copy: Two existing flows share one stable base: <speak>{{speakHotkey}}</speak> reads selected text aloud, <translate>{{translateHotkey}}</translate> captures selected text, translates it, and keeps the result visible in the UI.

overlayDock.compactSelection: Compact selected text and replace it
overlayDock.translateReplace: Translate selected text and replace it

status.default: Hover the edge glow to open the action bar.
status.settingsFailed: Could not open the settings page: {{detail}}
status.chatFailed: Could not toggle the chat window: {{detail}}
status.dictationPasteStarted: Hold to dictate. Release to paste the text.
status.dictationClipboardStarted: Hold to dictate. Release to save the text to clipboard.
status.compactDone: Selected text compacted and replaced ({{count}} characters).
status.compactFailed: Selected text could not be compacted: {{detail}}
status.translateReplaceDone: Selected text translated to {{language}} and replaced.
status.translateReplaceFailed: Selected text could not be translated: {{detail}}
status.languageFailed: Action language could not be changed: {{detail}}

dictationNotifications.listeningDetail: Speak now. Release the hotkey to finish.
dictationNotifications.pastedDetail: The transcription was pasted and is also in the clipboard.
dictationNotifications.clipboardDetail: The transcription is ready in the clipboard.

settings.title: Settings
settings.helper: Save applies changes to future hotkey runs and stores them in the local config file.
settings.aiProviderModeNote: Choose whether the assistant should call OpenAI directly with your own key or use your hosted backend account for realtime, translation, and speech generation.
settings.hostedModeScopeNote: Hosted mode now covers realtime voice, translation, and speech synthesis. Live STT and local wake-word detection still run on the device.
settings.speechPlaybackSpeedNote: 0.5x is slower, 1.0x is default, 2.0x is faster. This still applies to read-aloud and translate-plus-read output.
settings.uiLanguageNote: English is the source language for UI text, code comments, and documentation. German is provided through translation resources with English as the fallback.
settings.assistantCalibrationWarning: Please train the current name and language before saving.
settings.assistantCalibrationReady: Wake-word calibration is ready for this name and language.
settings.assistantNameNote: Use 3-8 characters, one single word. The wake phrase is just the assistant name: <wake>{{assistantName}}</wake> activates the assistant.
settings.voiceAssistantModelNote: Choose which realtime model the voice assistant session should use.
settings.voiceAssistantVoiceNote: Choose the voice that should be used for the selected realtime model. A voice is always required for both Hosted and direct OpenAI realtime sessions.
settings.voiceAssistantGenderNote: This affects the assistant's self-references and identity instructions, but no longer overrides the selected realtime voice.
settings.timerNotificationModeNote: Choose whether finished timers should trigger a repeating signal tone or a short voice reminder. Voice reminders briefly mute the microphone so they cannot be interrupted mid-notice.
settings.timerSignalToneNote: Pick the built-in sound that should loop when a timer finishes and the notification mode is set to signal tone. Dismiss the completed timer to stop the alert.
settings.dictationStatusNotificationsNote: Pulses the left action-bar handle in orange while dictating, then green or red when it finishes.
settings.dictationStatusNotificationsToggle: Show status pulses for dictation hotkeys
settings.activeTranscriptionLanguageNote: This language is also used while training and listening for the wake phrase. If you change it, you need to record the training samples again, for example de or en.
settings.wakeMatchThresholdNote: Higher is stricter. Recognition status shows the live wake score against this threshold.
settings.cueCooldownNote: Milliseconds to ignore repeated wake hits right after activation so one utterance cannot retrigger the assistant.
settings.launchAtLogin: Launch the app automatically when I sign in to Windows
settings.startHiddenOnLaunch: When started automatically, keep the window hidden and run in the background
settings.backgroundStartupNote: Saving this writes or removes a Windows Startup launcher for the current executable. Closing the main window now keeps the assistant alive in the tray so hotkeys, wake-word listening, and the realtime voice session can continue.
settings.actionBarDisplayNote: Choose how buttons appear in the action bar. Save settings to apply the change immediately.
settings.actionBarGlowColorNote: This color is used for the active glow on Speak, Chat, and Settings.
settings.hostedApiBaseUrlNote: Enter the Laravel app base URL, for example https://app.example.com. The desktop client uses it for sign-in and session minting.
settings.hostedApiBaseUrlManagedByEnv: This URL is managed through the project's .env file and is no longer edited in the settings form.
settings.hostedAccountSummaryDisconnected: Sign in to your Laravel backend to mint short-lived realtime session secrets without exposing the provider key to the client.
settings.hostedRealtimeReady: Hosted realtime access is available for the current subscription.
settings.hostedRealtimeUnavailable: The current subscription does not expose hosted realtime access yet.
settings.hostedWorkspaceNote: Leave this empty to use the backend current workspace. Select another workspace only if your account belongs to multiple teams.
settings.hostedLoginPageNote: Use the Login button in the top bar to sign in or sign out. Hosted mode and workspace selection only appear in Settings while you are signed in.
settings.hostedBillingNote: Select a plan and open Stripe Checkout in your default browser. The selected workspace determines whether the subscription applies to your personal workspace or a shared team workspace.
settings.openaiApiKeyNote: When set here, it overrides <env>OPENAI_API_KEY</env> from <envFile>.env</envFile>. Leave it empty to keep using <envFile>.env</envFile>.

dashboardHome.accountDisconnectedCopy: Use the Login button in the top bar to connect your hosted account. Hosted mode only becomes available in Settings after sign-in.

settingsPage.overviewDescription: On the left you only see the main categories. Click one of them and the matching dev settings appear on the right.
settingsPage.heroCopy: This page now keeps only the dev settings plus the theme selection from your design work.
settingsPage.unsavedChangesReady: Unsaved changes are ready to save.
settingsPage.designApplyNote: The selected theme is applied only after you save the settings.

general.label: General
general.description: Target language, UI language, and playback speed from dev.
general.helper: These are the language and playback settings that belong to dev.

assistant.label: Assistant
assistant.description: Wake name, voice identity, STT language, and matching settings from dev.
assistant.helper: Only the assistant naming, voice identity, training, and listening settings from dev.

startup.label: Startup
startup.description: Launch and background behavior from dev.
startup.helper: These are the startup and background options kept from dev.

api.label: API
api.description: Provider mode, hosted account, billing, and OpenAI access from dev.
api.helper: This keeps the dev provider flow: bring your own OpenAI key or sign in to the hosted backend.

authorization.label: Authorization
authorization.description: Provider mode, hosted account, billing, and OpenAI access from dev.
authorization.helper: This keeps the dev provider flow: bring your own OpenAI key or sign in to the hosted backend.

design.label: Design
design.description: Your theme system for dashboard, settings, action bar, and orb.
design.helper: These are the theme options from your design branches and nothing else.

actionbar.label: Actionbar
actionbar.title: Actionbar Display
actionbar.description: Action bar button display and behavior settings.
actionbar.helper: Control how the action bar buttons display text, icons, or both.

coral-companion.description: A clean light theme based on the web palette with bright surfaces, reef neutrals, warm coral accents, and the Coral Companion logo replacing the orb.

obsidian-halo.description: Deep black panels, bright white highlights, and a strong outer glow around frames and the orb.

shadow-satin.description: Graphite surfaces with softer silver edges for a calmer, more matte desktop look.

olympian-marble.description: White marble surfaces with fine dark veins, brushed gold framing, and cooler silver support accents.

retro-signal.description: A warm CRT-inspired retro look with amber glow, teal edge light, and subtle scanline texture.

fantasy-relic.description: Velvet night panels, enchanted jewel tones, and a rune-crystal orb instead of the black-hole core.

retro-arcade.description: Magenta-cyan arcade surfaces with a scanline pulse orb and animated level bars.

modern-glass.description: Clean frosted panels, cool blue highlights, and a minimal glass pulse orb with orbiting nodes.

universe-drift.description: Nebula gradients, deep midnight framing, and a planetary orbit orb with drifting satellites.

creed-eclipse.description: Animus-inspired holographic whites, icy cyan telemetry, and a synchronisation orb with scanning rings and data nodes.

volt-forge.description: Black alloy chassis, toxic volt tracers, and a reactor orb carved from hex steel and neon surge blades.

brass-engine.description: Riveted brass housings, furnace amber pressure lights, and a clockwork reactor orb wrapped in steam and gears.

shadow-monarch.description: Ink-black obsidian, abyssal indigo glows, and a shadow-gate orb with summoned shards and spectral smoke.

tsukuyomi-veil.description: Moonlit indigo lacquer, silver shrine accents, and a lunar eclipse orb wrapped in drifting veils and glyph petals.

anime-companion.description: A vivid 2D companion theme with sakura-pink neon, cyan sparkle trails, and a small animated character replacing the orb.

kitsune-matsuri.description: A sharp Japanese festival theme with vermilion lacquer frames, washi-gold glow, shoji motion, and a fox-mask voice overlay.

loginPage.title: Hosted account login
loginPage.copy: Sign in to your hosted account independently of the selected provider mode. The backend URL is configured centrally, and you can choose whether the app should start in Hosted or OpenAI key mode after sign-in.
loginPage.disconnectedCopy: No hosted account is connected yet.

debugPage.title: Debug
debugPage.copy: Technical status, diagnostics, feeds, and recent run details stay here so the main dashboard can remain focused on daily use.

assistantStatus.title: Wake / voice assistant
assistantStatus.assistantInactiveListening: Assistant is inactive and listening for the wake phrase.
assistantStatus.assistantInactiveMuted: Assistant is inactive. The realtime session stays online, but the microphone is muted.
assistantStatus.wakePhraseNote: WebView2 listens only for the wake phrase. The realtime WebRTC session stays connected in the background, and only the microphone uplink is toggled on activation.
assistantStatus.cueMatchingNote: Recognition status shows the current fuzzy wake score, component hints, and best matching fragment for tuning.
assistantStatus.noTranscriptPayload: No transcript payload for this event.

feeds.noToolCalls: No tool calls or background tasks yet.

runHistory.title: Recent run history
runHistory.metrics: hotkey->audio {{hotkeyToAudio}} ms - capture {{capture}} ms - capture->tts {{captureToTts}} ms - tts->audio {{ttsToAudio}} ms - audio->playback {{audioToPlayback}} ms

usage.title: Usage
usage.step1: Keep the app running in the background.
usage.step2: Live transcription starts automatically with the app and keeps local wake-word listening active via WebView2.
usage.step3: Say <wake>{{assistantWakePhrase}}</wake> or click <action>Activate assistant</action> to switch the realtime session to <mode>online_listening</mode> and enable the microphone uplink.
usage.step4: Let the assistant close itself naturally after the current turn, or use <hotkey>{{activateHotkey}}</hotkey> / <hotkey>{{deactivateHotkey}}</hotkey> or the buttons to return to <mode>online_muted</mode>.
usage.step5: Select text in another Windows app when you want to test the separate flows.
usage.step6: <speak>{{speakHotkey}}</speak> reads it aloud, while <translate>{{translateHotkey}}</translate> translates it and speaks the translation.

timers.sectionBody: Timers continue in the app background and can be managed by voice, from the dashboard, or from the action bar flyout.
timers.sectionHint: Pause, resume, rename, or delete timers without leaving the desktop flow.
timers.empty: No timers yet. Add one to start a countdown.

errors.signalStop: Timer signal could not be stopped: {{detail}}

dialogs.trainingNote: Live transcription is paused while calibration is open. Click Start, say the phrase, click Stop, then confirm or retry. Training currently uses {{language}}.
dialogs.reviewAfterStop: Stop the recording to review the captured phrase.
dialogs.resetBody: This restores every setting to its default value, including playback speed, translation language, the interface language, the stored API key, and any saved hosted sign-in token.
dialogs.unsavedSectionChangesBody: You have unsaved changes in {{category}}. If you switch categories now, those changes will be discarded.
dialogs.resetCategoryBody: Do you really want to reset the settings in {{category}} to their default values?
dialogs.resetAllSettingsBody: Do you really want to reset all settings in every category to their default values?
dialogs.voiceStyleRestartBody: Changing the voice assistant gender updates the identity instructions of the current realtime session.
dialogs.voiceStyleRestartDetail: To apply it cleanly, AIVA will first store compact memory from the current conversation, then restart the voice session, and continue with the new style in the background. Do you want to continue?
dialogs.voiceModelRestartBody: Changing the realtime model should not keep using the current realtime session.
dialogs.voiceModelRestartDetail: If you continue, AIVA saves the settings and closes the current voice session. The next time you activate the assistant or start live transcription, a new session is created with the selected model. Do you want to continue?
dialogs.voiceSelectionRestartBody: Changing the realtime voice should not keep using the current voice session after audio has already been emitted.
dialogs.voiceSelectionRestartDetail: If you continue, AIVA saves the settings and closes the current voice session. The next time you activate the assistant or start live transcription, a new session is created with the selected voice.
dialogs.voiceSessionConfigRestartBody: Changing multiple realtime session settings replaces the current session configuration.
dialogs.voiceSessionConfigRestartDetail: If you continue, AIVA saves the settings and closes the current voice session. The next time you activate the assistant or start live transcription, a new session is created with the updated model, voice, provider, and assistant configuration.
dialogs.voiceStyleModelRestartBody: Changing both the voice assistant gender and the realtime model replaces the current voice session configuration.
dialogs.voiceStyleModelRestartDetail: If you continue, AIVA saves the settings and closes the current voice session. The next time you activate the assistant or start live transcription, a new session is created with the new model and updated assistant style. Do you want to continue?
dialogs.voiceProviderRestartBody: Changing between Hosted and your own OpenAI key should not keep the current voice session running with the old provider.
dialogs.voiceProviderRestartDetail: If you continue, AIVA saves the settings and closes the current voice session. The next time you activate the assistant or start live transcription, a new session is created with the selected provider.
dialogs.voiceProviderCompositeRestartBody: Changing the provider together with other voice session settings replaces the current session configuration.
dialogs.voiceProviderCompositeRestartDetail: If you continue, AIVA saves the settings and closes the current voice session. The next time you activate the assistant or start live transcription, a new session is created with the updated provider and session settings.
dialogs.timerEditorBody: Choose a name and the countdown length. Leaving the name empty lets AIVA generate one automatically from the duration.
dialogs.timerEditorNameNote: Optional. If left empty, the timer is named from its duration, for example 15-minute timer.

training.stepSayNameAgain: Please say only the name again:
training.startHint: Click Start, speak the shown phrase, then click Stop.
training.runtimeUnavailable: SpeechRecognition is not available in this runtime.
training.noTranscriptCaptured: No transcript captured yet. Please retry.
training.completed: Wake-word calibration completed. Save settings to persist the recorded samples.
training.capturedSaved: Assistant calibration captured. Save settings to persist it.
training.nextSample: Sample saved. Continue with the next recording.

voiceRuntime.liveTranscriptionStartsAutomatically: Live transcription starts automatically when the app launches.
voiceRuntime.persistentSessionStarting: Persistent realtime voice session is starting.
voiceRuntime.assistantInactiveMicrophoneMuted: Assistant inactive. The realtime session is still connected, but the microphone is muted ({{source}}).
voiceRuntime.wakeListenerStopped: Wake-word listener stopped. The realtime session remains connected and muted.
voiceRuntime.liveTranscriptionStopped: Live transcription is stopped. The realtime session remains connected and muted.
voiceRuntime.failedToWriteSttLog: Failed to write STT debug log: {{detail}}

liveStt.speechRecognitionUnavailable: SpeechRecognition is not available in this WebView2 runtime.

languagePolicy.title: Language policy
languagePolicy.description: Code comments, documentation, translation keys, and fallback UI copy must be written in English. Localized user-facing text belongs in translation resources, not in inline source strings.
